title: pulmonaria vallarsae subsp. apennina
intro: Pulmonaria vallarsae subsp. apennina is a subspecies of the lungwort plant, a perennial herb native to the Apennine Mountains in Italy. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of saponins and glycosides. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as relieving coughs and respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antiviral and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Pulmonaria vallarsae subsp. apennina are typically made from the leaves and flowers, and can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infused oil.
